cards.prompt: Array covariance lets an object[] variable point at a string[], but the CLR type-checks every store at run time. Writing an int (42) into an array whose real element type is string is rejected, so the catch fires and it prints ArrayTypeMismatchException
cards.code: using System; object[] a = new string[1]; try { a[0] = 42; Console.WriteLine("no throw"); } catch (ArrayTypeMismatchException) { Console.WriteLine("ArrayTypeMismatchException"); }

cards.prompt: An array has its own type object distinct from the element type: the CLR auto-manufactures a distinct array type per element type, so new int[3].GetType() prints System.Int32[] (not Int32 or System.Array)
The CLR bounds-checks every index. An array of length 3 has valid indices 0..2; index 3 is out of range, so the catch fires and it prints IndexOutOfRangeException
cards.code: using System; var a = new int[3]; try { Console.WriteLine(a[3]); } catch (IndexOutOfRangeException) { Console.WriteLine("IndexOutOfRangeException"); }
